extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the stage 3 sprint emphatically after opening his sprint with 100m remaining, beating THEUNS Edward by multiple bike lengths.
tactical_patterns: BENNETT Sam delayed sprint until 100m, suggesting superiority allows late acceleration when other sprinters already committed at speed.
Team Sunweb lost WALSCHEID Max in final 2km despite positioning, revealing their lead-out lacks horsepower in chaotic finishes.
DEGENKOLB John positioned through DRUCKER Jempy with 2km out, showing Bora-Hansgrohe timed approach better than rivals who went early.
No single team controlled final 2km despite multiple trains present, indicating mixed positioning neutralizes traditional lead-out advantages.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Demonstrated clear superiority by waiting until 100m to open sprint and still winning by multiple lengths
rider_signals.THEUNS Edward: positive: Strong second place despite large gap to winner suggests consistent sprint positioning skills
rider_signals.WALSCHEID Max: negative: Lost positioning in final 2km despite Sunweb lead-out effort, indicating vulnerability when chaos disrupts trains
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: positive: Bora-Hansgrohe timing through DRUCKER showed tactical discipline compared to teams committing too early
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: neutral: Defended race lead through sprint stage positioning, showing veteran craft despite being climber type
